title: MLflow Custom View Catalog
description: Custom A2UI catalog for the model trace explorer custom view. Extends the basic catalog's Text, Row, and Column components with MLflow-specific MediaRenderer, Card, Icon, StatCard, DataTable, TimelineChart, TreeView, TreeNode, Markdown, AssessmentBoard, AssessmentCard, KeyValueViewer, and FeedbackButtons components.

MediaRenderer.description: Renders trace media (image, audio, or PDF). Accepts a direct http(s):// URL, a data: URI, or an mlflow-attachment:// URI. For mlflow-attachment:// URIs the blob is fetched from the trace artifact store and rendered inline (image/audio/PDF are dispatched by content type, with a download fallback for large blobs and a click-to-expand preview for images). Audio and PDFs always arrive as mlflow-attachment:// URIs; only images can be a direct URL.

url.description: The media source: an http(s):// URL, a data: URI, or an mlflow-attachment:// URI.

weight.description: Relative flex weight when placed directly inside a Row/Column.

Card.description: A bordered container around a SINGLE child, themed with Databricks Design System tokens (a slightly-darker-than-white surface, border, and rounded corners) so it sits natively in the MLflow UI. To display multiple elements, wrap them in a Row/Column and pass that container's id as the child.

child.description: The id of the single child component to render inside the card.

weight.description: Relative flex weight when placed directly inside a Row/Column.

Icon.description: A single Databricks Design System icon, referenced by name. Accepts the basic catalog's Material-style names (e.g. "check", "close", "download", "search", "settings", "warning", "error", "info", "person", "folder", "star") which are mapped to the closest DS icon, plus DS-native aliases (e.g. "trash", "gear", "pencil", "tag"). Unknown names degrade gracefully to a neutral default icon. Use sparingly: most components (StatCard/DataTable/etc.) already carry their own icon.

name.description: The icon to display, e.g. "check" or "warning".

size.description: Icon size in pixels. Defaults to the inherited font size.

StatCard.description: A metric card displaying a value, a caption, and a tinted icon. Matches the tool-call statistics cards on the experiment overview page.

value.description: The metric value to display, e.g. "14" or "275.71ms".

icon.description: The icon to display next to the value.

tone.description: The color tone applied to the icon.

DataTable.description: A generic, domain-agnostic table. Callers describe the columns and supply rows whose cells are positionally aligned to the columns. An optional per-row color renders a leading indicator dot in the first cell.

title.description: Optional heading shown above the table.

icon.description: Optional icon shown next to the title.

color.description: Optional CSS color for a leading indicator dot.

cells.description: Cell values, positionally aligned to the columns.

emptyMessage.description: Text shown when there are no rows.

TimelineChart.description: A generic Gantt-style timeline. Each row is a horizontal bar positioned by its start/end offsets (in milliseconds, relative to a common origin). The component computes the axis, scaling, and duration labels, and works for any number of rows. Mirrors the Details & Timeline gantt view.

title.description: Optional heading shown above the chart.

icon.description: Optional icon shown next to the title.

rows.description: The timeline rows, in display order.

label.description: The row label (e.g. the span name).

color.description: Optional CSS color for the bar.

emptyMessage.description: Text shown when there are no rows.

TreeView.description: A collapsible tree container. It lays out its TreeNode children (referenced by id) on the left and, when a node with panelItems is selected, shows that node's side panel (built by the host from the span's data) on the right. Selection is also driven by markdown #span:<id> deeplinks inside any markdown. Compose TreeNodes to map 1:1 to spans (with an input/output side panel) or to represent higher-level milestones whose markdown links to spans.

title.description: Optional heading shown above the tree.

children.description: The root TreeNode component ids, in display order.

emptyMessage.description: Text shown when there are no nodes.

TreeNode.description: A composable tree node. Renders a row (icon + label + optional badge) and an optional set of nested child TreeNodes. Attach panelItems directives describing what the host should build in the TreeView side panel when the node is selected (the span's input/output/attributes, a markdown summary, feedback buttons) — the host fills in the heavy span data, so the node stays tiny.

label.description: The node label (e.g. the span name). Used when title is absent.

title.description: Preferred node heading; falls back to label.

icon.description: The span icon type (matches the Details & Timeline icons).

hasException.description: Renders the icon in the danger tone with an exception marker.

isRootSpan.description: Renders the icon in the root/AI tone.

badge.description: Optional trailing tag text (e.g. an assessment count).

spanId.description: Optional span id this node represents. Lets markdown #span:<id> deeplinks target this node and scopes the side panel + embedded feedback.

panelItems.description: Directives for the side panel shown when this node is selected. The host builds the actual components (KeyValueViewer / Markdown / FeedbackButtons) from the span data — do NOT emit the span data yourself.

type.description: input/output/attributes -> the span field as a KeyValueViewer; markdown -> a Markdown block; feedback -> FeedbackButtons scoped to this node's span.

title.description: Section heading (markdown) or value label override.

children.description: Nested TreeNode ids, rendered indented below this node.

Markdown.description: Renders a markdown text block (optionally under a title). Links of the form [text](#span:<spanId>) select the TreeView node representing that span instead of navigating, so a span summary can deeplink into the spans it references. Typically built by the host inside a TreeNode side panel, but usable anywhere.

text.description: The markdown body. Supports [text](#span:<spanId>) deeplinks when inside a TreeView.

title.description: Optional heading shown above the markdown.

KeyValueViewer.description: Displays a SINGLE labeled value using the Details & Timeline snippet renderer, with a format toggle (text/json/markdown for strings; an interactive JSON tree for objects/arrays). Use it to show one span attribute/input/output field directly.

label.description: The key/label shown above the value, e.g. the attribute name.

value.description: JSON-encoded value to display (object/array -> JSON tree, string -> text/markdown/json).

initialFormat.description: Initial display format for string values.

hideFormatToggle.description: When true, hides the per-value format dropdown.

AssessmentCard.description: A single assessment box: the category is the header, the verdict is a colored badge, and the rationale fills the body. Sentiment drives the color (green positive, red negative/error). Reusable — append one per assessment into an AssessmentBoard (or any Row/Column).

name.description: The assessment category, shown as the box header.

value.description: The verdict/value, shown as a colored badge.

rationale.description: The explanation shown in the box body.

AssessmentBoard.description: A wrapping container for assessment boxes. Renders an optional title followed by a wrapping row of child component ids (typically one AssessmentCard per assessment). Add more assessments by appending more AssessmentCard children.

title.description: Optional heading shown above the boxes.

icon.description: Optional icon shown next to the title.

children.description: The card component ids to lay out in a wrapping row.

emptyMessage.description: Text shown when there are no cards.

FeedbackButtons.description: An interactive thumbs up / thumbs down control. Clicking a thumb logs an MLflow feedback assessment (boolean: thumbs up = true, thumbs down = false) for the current trace and reflects the choice via its bound value. A lightweight alternative to the full assessment input form.

label.description: Optional prompt shown next to the thumbs, e.g. "Was this helpful?".

name.description: The assessment name to log. Defaults to "User feedback".

value.description: Selected state: true = thumbs up, false = thumbs down. Bind to a /feedback/... data-model path to reflect and seed the choice.

spanId.description: Optional span id to scope the feedback to a specific span instead of the whole trace.

weight.description: Relative flex weight when placed directly inside a Row/Column.
